rationale: The citing paper uses Dhar and Wertenbroch (2000) as part of the core prior literature defining hedonic-versus-utilitarian choice, not just as a peripheral reference. It explicitly states that its studies follow prior binary-choice paradigms in this stream (alongside Shiv and Fedorikhin), and positions its contribution as adding to that literature by introducing interface effects. Although the citation appears in grouped form, it plays a clear conceptual and design-framing role in how the paper motivates and situates its hypotheses and studies.
relation_description: The 2016 paper builds on Dhar and Wertenbroch's hedonic-versus-utilitarian choice literature as background for its own theory and study design. It uses that prior line as a foundation, then adds computer interface direct-touch as a new antecedent affecting hedonic choice.
